--------- STM32 PROJECTS ----------

1- 
--> I configured PA5 pin as "GPIO_Output". The point of this is giving info to STM32. Controlling the voltage on PA5. It can output either HIGH(3.3V) or LOW(0V).
--> CubeMX: When we selected PA5->GPIO_Output in the .ioc file, CubeMX automatically generated all the inilization code.
--> HAL_GPIO_TogglePin(GPIOA, GPIO_PIN_5); This code block does; pin is high ? it becomes low : high. Since the led is connected to PA5, the led turns on and off repeatedly.
--> HAL_Delay(500); This tells the microcontroller LED ON, wait 500ms, LED OFF, wait 500ms, repeat forever.
--> Why does the program keep running? cause of while(1). 

2-
--> Connected an external led on a breadboard. (used 5mm led, 220 ohm r, two jumper)
--> Pin name PA5 is an arduino code name. refer to stm32, its name is SCK/D13 pin
--> When connecting the ground jumper, DONT FORGET TO CONNECT IN STM32'S GROUND PIN, not in breadboard ground. (picture is included in the same folder)

3-
-->Controlling a led on the card using button(external or b1 user button) (used button, two jumper)
--> Used PC13 or PA0(for external button) as a GPIO input to read the button. Configured PA0 as GPIO input and selected pull-up for keeping pin to HIGH
--> Read the button state using (HAL_GPIO_ReadPin(GPIOA, GPIO_PIN_0) and controlled the led using if-else statement
--> Is PA0 low(0)? Because of we use the pull-up; when we push the button, PA0 is LOW(RESET) otherwise PA0 is HIGH(SET). if only runs when we push the button.

if(HAL_GPIO_ReadPin(GPIOA, GPIO_PIN_0)== GPIO_PIN_RESET){
  HAL_GPIO_WritePin(GPIOA, GPIO_PIN_5, GPIO_PIN_RESET); (turns the led off)
}
else{
  HAL_GPIO_WritePin(GPIOA, GPIO_PIN_5, GPIO_PIN_SET);}   (turns the led on)

--> RESET means LOW(0), SET means HIGH(1)
    Press the button : led turns off
    Release the button : led turns on
    
4-
--> Controlling the brightness of an led (used led, two jumper, 220 ohm resistor)
--> Used Timer 2(TIM2) to generate PWM signal, connected to PA0.
--> Set Channel 1 to PWM Generation CH1
        Prescaler to 83
        Counter 999
--> HAL_TIM_PWM_Start(&htim2, TIM_CHANNEL_1); starts pwm.
--> __HAL_TIM_SET_COMPARE(&htim2, TIM_CHANNEL_1,i); set the duty cycle.
--> Through the first for loop the brightness of led is increasing slowly, on second for loop decreasing slowly.
--> HAL_Delay(1); wait 1 ms.
--> we used the "uint16_t" parameter. Meaning of this is unsigned int, 16 bit, and t(type). It is totally suitable for PWM.


5.
--> We used the user button(PC13) to generate an external interrupt(EXTI) and toggle the onboard LD2 led(PA5). (No external wiring)
--> Unlike previous projects, the CPU doesn't continuously check the button. Instead, the interrupt automatically notifies the processor when the button is pressed.
--> We configured PA5 to GPIO_Output, PC13 to GPIO_EXTI13. Enableb NVIC(Nested Vector Interrupt Controller) setting.
--> Previously we used while(1) loop.
--> Now interrupt. This is how interrupt works: CPU waits > Button pressed > EXTI interrupt occurs > HAL automatically calls. HAL_GPIO_EXTI_Callback() > Execution returns to while(1)
--> HAL_GPIO_EXTI_Callback() When interrupt exists, it works.
